name: Malaysia
intro: Malaysia's project market has two engines: Kuala Lumpur's banking and shared-services towers, and the manufacturing and energy economy stretching from Penang's electronics belt to Johor's data-centre boom. Global firms have moved regional delivery hubs to Malaysia for cost and talent reasons, and those hubs hire against international benchmarks — PMP and CSM are the credentials they list. HRD Corp claimable training culture means Malaysian employers actively fund certification. Our live online weekend batch runs on the IST schedule, converting to comfortable afternoon-to-night hours in Malaysia, so the whole workshop fits a single weekend. Teaching is in English with an international cohort and invoices suit employer claims. After class you get exam application guidance, realistic mock exams, and open access to the trainer for doubts until you pass.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Malaysia?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Malaysia?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Malaysia.
